text: Start writing, no matter what. The water does not flow until the faucet is turned on.
author: Louis L'Amour
zagafyPhrase: The faucet is already in your hand.

text: You can always edit a bad page. You can't edit a blank page.
author: Jodi Picoult
zagafyPhrase: The page is waiting. It doesn't judge.

text: The first draft is just you telling yourself the story.
author: Terry Pratchett
zagafyPhrase: Nobody else needs to hear it yet.

text: I write only when inspiration strikes. Fortunately it strikes every morning at nine o'clock sharp.
author: W. Somerset Maugham
zagafyPhrase: You showed up. That is the hardest part.

text: Almost all good writing begins with terrible first efforts.
author: Anne Lamott
zagafyPhrase: Terrible is the beginning of something real.

text: You don't start out writing good stuff. You start out writing crap and thinking it's good stuff, and then gradually you get better at it.
author: Octavia E. Butler
zagafyPhrase: Getting better starts with getting started.

text: I can fix a bad page. I can't fix a blank page.
author: Nora Roberts
zagafyPhrase: One imperfect sentence is all it takes.

text: The scariest moment is always just before you start.
author: Stephen King
zagafyPhrase: You're already past the scariest part.

text: Writing is like driving at night in the fog. You can only see as far as your headlights, but you can make the whole trip that way.
author: E. L. Doctorow
zagafyPhrase: The next few feet are enough.

text: I don't know where I'm going, but I'm on my way.
author: Carl Sandburg
zagafyPhrase: The path appears as you walk it.

text: If you wait for inspiration to write, you're not a writer, you're a waiter.
author: Dan Poynter
zagafyPhrase: You chose to write. That makes you a writer.

text: Close the door. Write with no one looking over your shoulder.
author: Barbara Kingsolver
zagafyPhrase: This space is yours alone.

text: Don't get it right, just get it written.
author: James Thurber
zagafyPhrase: Written beats right. Every time.

text: The desire to write grows with writing.
author: Erasmus
zagafyPhrase: Start small. The rest will follow.

text: You might not write well every day, but you can always edit a bad page. You can't edit a blank page.
author: Jodi Picoult
zagafyPhrase: Even a rough page is progress.

text: When I sit down to write a book, I do not say to myself, 'I am going to produce a work of art.' I write it because there is some lie that I want to expose.
author: George Orwell
zagafyPhrase: What truth is waiting inside your story?

text: I went for years not finishing anything. Because, of course, when you finish something you can be judged.
author: Erica Jong
zagafyPhrase: Finishing is an act of courage.

text: There is no greater agony than bearing an untold story inside you.
author: Maya Angelou
source: I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings
zagafyPhrase: Your story needs you to tell it.

text: We are all apprentices in a craft where no one ever becomes a master.
author: Ernest Hemingway
zagafyPhrase: Mastery is a direction, not a destination.

text: If the book is true, it will find an audience that is meant to read it.
author: Wally Lamb
zagafyPhrase: Write what is true to you.

text: A writer is someone for whom writing is more difficult than it is for other people.
author: Thomas Mann
zagafyPhrase: Difficulty is not a sign of failure.

text: You can't think yourself out of a writing block; you have to write yourself out of a thinking block.
author: John Rogers
zagafyPhrase: The words will untangle the thoughts.

text: Rest is not idleness, and to lie sometimes on the grass under trees on a summer's day, listening to the murmur of the water, is by no means a waste of time.
author: John Lubbock
zagafyPhrase: Rest is part of the work.

text: When you're exhausted, stop. When you're rested, write. There is no heroism in burnout.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
zagafyPhrase: Gentleness is not weakness.

text: I learned that you should feel when writing, not like Lord Byron on a mountain top, but like a child stringing beads in kindergarten — happy, absorbed and quietly putting one bead on after another.
author: Brenda Ueland
zagafyPhrase: One bead at a time.

text: Tomorrow may be hell, but today was a good writing day, and on the good writing days nothing else matters.
author: Neil Gaiman
zagafyPhrase: Today is the day that matters.

text: Not knowing what comes next is the condition of the creative life.
author: John McPhee
zagafyPhrase: Uncertainty is where stories come alive.

text: The beautiful part of writing is that you don't have to get it right the first time, unlike, say, a brain surgeon.
author: Robert Cormier
zagafyPhrase: Revision exists so you can be free.

text: Writing a novel is like driving a car at night. You can see only as far as your headlights, but you can make the whole trip that way.
author: E. L. Doctorow
zagafyPhrase: Trust the next few words.

text: You don't have to be great to start, but you have to start to be great.
author: Zig Ziglar
zagafyPhrase: Starting is the first act of greatness.

text: I write to discover what I know.
author: Flannery O'Connor
zagafyPhrase: The answer is inside the writing.

text: All you have to do is write one true sentence. Write the truest sentence that you know.
author: Ernest Hemingway
zagafyPhrase: One true sentence. That's all.

text: Writing is its own reward.
author: Henry Miller
zagafyPhrase: The act itself is the gift.

text: If I waited for perfection, I would never write a word.
author: Margaret Atwood
zagafyPhrase: Perfection is the enemy of your story.

text: The only way out is through.
author: Robert Frost
zagafyPhrase: Through is one word at a time.

text: I do not over-intellectualize the production process. I try to keep it simple: Tell the damned story.
author: Tom Clancy
zagafyPhrase: Your story doesn't need to be clever. It needs to be told.

text: Whatever you do, don't stop writing. Keep going, even if it's terrible.
author: Sylvia Plath
zagafyPhrase: Terrible is temporary. Silence is louder.

text: When I'm writing, I know I'm doing the thing I was born to do.
author: Anne Sexton
zagafyPhrase: You were made for this.

text: The story will tell you where to go. You just have to listen.
author: Toni Morrison
zagafyPhrase: Listen. Your characters know the way.

text: Being a writer is a very peculiar sort of a job: it's always you versus a blank sheet of paper and quite often the blank sheet wins.
author: Neil Gaiman
zagafyPhrase: Today, you win.

text: I can shake off everything as I write; my sorrows disappear, my courage is reborn.
author: Anne Frank
source: The Diary of a Young Girl
zagafyPhrase: Writing is how you find your strength.

text: Write what should not be forgotten.
author: Isabel Allende
zagafyPhrase: What must your reader remember?

text: A professional writer is an amateur who didn't quit.
author: Richard Bach
zagafyPhrase: You haven't quit. You're here.

text: One day I will find the right words, and they will be simple.
author: Jack Kerouac
zagafyPhrase: Simple is waiting for you.

text: Fill your paper with the breathings of your heart.
author: William Wordsworth
zagafyPhrase: Your heart already knows what to say.

text: I think all writing is a disease. You can't stop it.
author: William Carlos Williams
zagafyPhrase: The urge is still there. Trust it.

text: Imagination is the beginning of creation. You imagine what you desire, you will what you imagine, and at last you create what you will.
author: George Bernard Shaw
zagafyPhrase: Imagine the next scene. Then write it.

text: The act of writing is the act of discovering what you believe.
author: David Hare
zagafyPhrase: Belief comes from writing, not before it.

text: Every secret of a writer's soul, every experience of his life, every quality of his mind, is written large in his works.
author: Virginia Woolf
zagafyPhrase: Your soul is the story's greatest strength.

text: I love deadlines. I love the whooshing noise they make as they go by.
author: Douglas Adams
zagafyPhrase: There is no deadline here. Only you and your words.

text: Amateurs sit and wait for inspiration, the rest of us just get up and go to work.
author: Stephen King
zagafyPhrase: You got up. Now write.

text: Every first draft is perfect because all the first draft has to do is exist.
author: Jane Smiley
zagafyPhrase: Existence is the only bar to clear.

text: To gain your own voice, you have to forget about having it heard.
author: Allen Ginsberg
zagafyPhrase: Write for no one. Find everyone.

text: I have been successful probably because I have always realized that I knew nothing about writing and have merely tried to tell an interesting story entertainingly.
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
zagafyPhrase: Tell the story. That's all there is.

text: I would advise anyone who aspires to a writing career that before developing his talent he would be wise to develop a thick skin.
author: Harper Lee
zagafyPhrase: Your skin grows thicker with every word.

text: Write freely and as rapidly as possible and throw the whole thing on paper. Never correct or rewrite until the whole thing is down.
author: John Steinbeck
zagafyPhrase: Down first. Perfect later.

text: I do not know what I think until I write it down.
author: Joan Didion
zagafyPhrase: The writing knows before you do.

text: The pages are still blank, but there is a miraculous feeling of the words being there, written in invisible ink and clamoring to become visible.
author: Vladimir Nabokov
zagafyPhrase: The words are already there. Let them through.

text: Easy reading is damn hard writing.
author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
zagafyPhrase: Hard writing is honest work.

text: Sleep on your writing; take a walk over it; scrutinize it of a morning; review it of an afternoon; digest it after a meal; let it sleep in your drawer a twelvemonth.
author: A. Bronson Alcott
zagafyPhrase: Sometimes the best writing happens after rest.
